items.title: U.S. says it targeted Iranian forces after attacks that killed two American service members
items.summary: Centcom says it hit Iran's coastal surveillance and air defense facilities a day after two U.S. troops were killed in an Iranian strike on Jordan.

items.title: American Airlines CEO lays out his vision to close a more than $3 billion profit gap
items.summary: The carrier is working on improving reliability, investing in more premium seats and lounges, and considering Boeing and Airbus for a new wide-body plane order.

items.title: Cybersecurity risks posed by over-the-air tech in autos has analysts concerned
items.summary: The automotive industry's increasing use of over-the-air technology makes it more susceptible to cyberattacks, analysts say.

items.title: Housing costs is the top political issue for young voters, CNBC survey finds
items.summary: The cost of housing is the top priority for voters ages 18 to 34, besting food and healthcare cost concerns, the CNBC All-America Economic Survey found.

items.title: Kalshi adds 3 million new users as prediction market capitalizes on the World Cup
items.summary: The prediction market has launched some ads featuring soccer players, as well as formed a partnership to display its brand in more places during the tournament.

items.title: Top Wall Street analysts suggest these 3 dividend stocks for steady income
items.summary: Here are three dividend-paying stocks that are highlighted by Wall Street's top pros, as tracked by TipRanks.

items.title: Taylor Farms recalls iceberg lettuce in 27 states due to cyclosporiasis outbreak
items.summary: Taylor Farms is recalling iceberg lettuce in 27 states because of the potential that the greens could be contaminated with cyclosporiasis.

items.title: 'WarshGPT': How Wall Street is adapting to the Fed's new era of communication
items.summary: Investment firms are readying for an era with less public commentary from the U.S. central bank. Some are relying on AI for an edge.

items.title: World Cup audience stayed bipartisan despite Trump's prominent role, CNBC survey finds
items.summary: Nearly half of registered voters watched the 2026 World Cup, with income and education shaping viewership more than party affiliation, the survey found.

items.title: Why Dana White thinks much more of UFC's future will be Power Slap and boxing
items.summary: UFC president and CEO Dana White predicted that Power Slap may become as big as his flagship fighting sport at the Game Plan conference in New York.

items.title: AI may make you 'boring' at work, Columbia Business School professor says: How it can hurt your career
items.summary: Using AI at work in the wrong ways could lead to "gradually falling behind and becoming complacent," said Sandra Matz, a professor at Columbia Business School.

items.title: Scam victims can owe taxes on stolen money. A bill in Congress could offer relief
items.summary: A bill in the House would restore a pre-2018 rule that generally allowed a tax deduction for theft losses and would add other relief for fraud victims.

items.title: Stop asking 'How was work today?' Successful, mentally strong couples ask these 7 questions instead
items.summary: This question can get you "stuck in a communication rut," says Amy Morin, author of "13 Things Mentally Strong Couples Don't Do." Here's what to ask instead.

items.title: California's billionaire tax: What's at risk for the biggest state economy in America
items.summary: California's billionaire tax would generate quick revenue, but Norway's example shows the longer-term economic impact of targeting the wealthy is unclear.

items.title: Fanatics CEO Michael Rubin on two sports businesses his multibillion-dollar conglomerate won't enter
items.summary: Fanatics has become one of the most influential companies in sports, building out a massive merchandise, collectibles and sports gambling platform.

items.title: The AI rotation stole the spotlight from a strong start to earnings season
items.summary: The AI trade dominated the market once again this week, upstaging an impressive start to the second-quarter earnings season.

items.title: Two unexpected revelations in Warren Buffett's CNBC interview
items.summary: Warren Buffett tells CNBC that Bill Gates' ties to Jeffrey Epstein didn't drive his termination of donations.

items.title: Analysis: Fed Chairman Warsh faces an inflation credibility test after Congress hearings
items.summary: The new Fed chairman avoided major stumbles in two days of testimony before the House and Senate, but faces a rapid test of his commitment to price stability.

items.title: Korean beauty products are becoming mainstream in the U.S. Why there may be even more growth ahead
items.summary: Morgan Stanley forecasts that K-beauty sales in the U.S. can reach approximately $4 billion in 2026.

items.title: How are prediction markets taxed? The IRS hasn’t provided guidance yet
items.summary: Experts say the lack of federal guidance makes it's unclear on how prediction markets winnings should be reported and levied.

items.title: Trump rips Canada as wildfire smoke spreads, says U.S. will add pollution cost to tariffs
items.summary: The smoke coming from active wildfires in Canada has led to unhealthy air quality in swaths of the U.S., which could affect the World Cup final in New Jersey.

items.title: Democratic socialists top MAGA candidates among voters in CNBC's All-America poll
items.summary: The survey's findings come as democratic socialist candidates win Democratic primaries following the 2025 election of New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani.

items.title: The White House is dictating access to frontier AI models, shifting power from tech giants, sources say
items.summary: The Trump administration is taking steps to control who gets access to the latest frontier models, people familiar with the matter told CNBC.

items.title: Inside the Chinese fraud rings stealing billions from banks and retailers
items.summary: Chinese organized crime groups are making as much as $1 billion annually in tap-to-pay fraud schemes targeting retailers and banks.

items.title: Epstein victims blast Trump attorney general nominee Todd Blanche after meeting
items.summary: "Todd Blanche treated the meeting as a mere 'check-the-box' exercise intended to secure votes for his confirmation," said Epstein survivor Dani Bensky.

items.title: 27-year-old locksmith spent $7,000 on tools, works around the clock in NYC: 'I'd never held a screwdriver in my life' before starting
items.summary: Yona Sahar, founder of Locksmith Girl of NYC, spent two years learning the trade. Now, she runs her own business while working around the clock.

items.title: Taco Bell says it has removed lettuce linked to cyclosporiasis outbreak from its restaurants
items.summary: Taco Bell, which the CDC linked to the cyclosporiasis outbreak, will likely recover soon from the health safety scare, according to analysts.

items.title: Economic outlook is worsening and Trump is getting blamed, CNBC survey finds
items.summary: The public is as depressed about the economy as it has been since the years just after the pandemic, according to the All-America Economic Survey.

items.title: I left the U.S. for Trinidad and Tobago: My family of 4 spends about $3,000 a month—and we've never been happier
items.summary: Chantel Henry lives with her husband and two kids in Trinidad. She says living there has made her American Dream more affordable.

items.title: Leather jacket worn by Nvidia CEO goes for just under $1 million at Sotheby's auction
items.summary: The high price for the garment is a sign that collectors are looking to bid on artifacts and collectibles from the artificial intelligence boom.
